questions.question: The author includes several examples of Bourke-White being the “first” or “only” person to do something. Why do you think the author chose to include these details?

responses.content: (4) Her mother gave Margaret her first camera in 1921, when she was 17 years old. Her interest in photography grew as a result of her father’s enthusiasm for cameras. A few years later, Margaret’s classmates at Cornell University became her first admirers when photos she took of the campus appeared in the school newspapers. A year after graduating, Margaret moved to Cleveland, Ohio and opened a commercial photography studio.
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's highlight is from paragraph 4, which describes how Margaret Bourke-White received her first camera and began her interest in photography. However, the question specifically asks for a sentence from paragraphs 8-10 that shows Bourke-White accomplishing something no one else had done before. The highlighted text does not meet this requirement, as it does not describe any of her groundbreaking achievements. Therefore, the response is incorrect.

responses.content: (8) In 1929, Henry Luce invited Margaret to work at Fortune Magazine. She jumped at the chance and became the first woman in a new field called photojournalism, in which photographers reported the news through images.

She jumped at the chance and became the first woman in a new field called photojournalism, in which photographers reported the news through images.' The highlight is within the character limit and directly answers the question by showing Bourke-White accomplishing something no one else had done before. Therefore, the response is correct.
